title: Telemetry, Capacity, Scheduling, Deployment, Backup, and Recovery · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers telemetry, capacity, scheduling, deployment, backup, and recovery into a changed operable recoverable Go crawler service. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of operable recoverable Go crawler service with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Correlate run, policy, URL key, attempt, response, parser, frontier, goroutine, report, and recovery while redacting content and bounding labels.
Measure CPU, heap, allocations, goroutines, open files, sockets, queue age, bytes, throughput, and origin fairness under representative fixtures.
Use one schedule owner, overlap lock, missed-run policy, configuration and artifact identity, owner pause, signal handling, and clean shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Causal Observability
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 6100 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile telemetry, capacity, durable schedules, immutable deployment, backup, restore, and safe resume; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Correlate run, policy, URL key, attempt, response, parser, frontier, goroutine, report, and recovery while redacting content and bounding labels.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Causal Observability.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Backup Restore · changed case · Lab Backup Restore, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Backup Restore precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Backup Restore · changed case · Lab Backup Restore, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A lab Go team handles case 5386 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile telemetry, capacity, durable schedules, immutable deployment, backup, restore, and safe resume; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Set RPO, RTO, retention, encrypted backup, isolated restore, offline reconciliation, secret rotation, and safe-resume evidence for each data class.

options.text: Set RPO, RTO, retention, encrypted backup, isolated restore, offline reconciliation, secret rotation, and safe-resume evidence for each data class. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for operable recoverable Go crawler service.
A successful copy command proves recoverable crawler state. That claim does not survive the backup restore · changed case evidence boundary.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Backup Restore
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-backup-restore-qxa93s" that uses case 4056 to reconcile telemetry, capacity, durable schedules, immutable deployment, backup, restore, and safe resume. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Backup Restore.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Backup Restore into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Backup Restore, keep Backup Restore connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Backup Restore
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 2148 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile telemetry, capacity, durable schedules, immutable deployment, backup, restore, and safe resume; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Set RPO, RTO, retention, encrypted backup, isolated restore, offline reconciliation, secret rotation, and safe-resume evidence for each data class.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
